LAW AND MOTION 302, AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM MAY-08-2012 TO LAW AND MOTION AT MAY-17-2012, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 NO LATER THAN 4PM MAY 9, 2012 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA CSR # 5153. (302)

ALL FUTURE MOTIONS IN THIS CASE SHALL BE CALENDARED FOR DEPARTMENT 501. MOVING PARTY SHALL PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 501 AS REQUIRED BY SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6C NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 30, 2012. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: DIANA CHEN, CSR # 11312

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT DENNIS MITCHELL'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. FURTHER THE COURT FINDS PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT CONDUCT TO AWARD PUNITIVE DAMAGES. COURT SIGNS ORDER SUBMITTED WITH THE PLEADINGS. JUDGE SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. SMITH, NOT REPORTED

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT DENNIS MITCHELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE FOLLOWING PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT ARE STRICKEN THAT REFERENCE TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES: PAGE, 3 PARAGRAPH 14, LINES 25-25; PAGE 5, PARAGRAPH 25, LINE 14; PAGE 6, PARAGRAPH 28, LINE 4; AND PAGE 6, PARAGRAPH, LINE 14. JUDGE SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. SMITH, NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DENNIS MITCHELL'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, ASSAULT, AND VIOLATION OF THE SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT ORDINANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL THREE CLAIMS. EACH OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ALLEGES INSUFFICIENT ULTIMATE FACTS, YET IT IS POSSIBLE THAT PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO CURE THESE DEFICIENCIES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Dec-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Austin W. Manes (650-637-9100) of Hayes Scott Bonino Ellingson & McLay for Dennis L. Mitchell defendant and moving party; Christine A. Chorney (415-824-7055) plaintiff and opposing party in Pro Per

ARGUED - The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: DEFENDANT DENNIS MITCHELL's DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff failed to allege outrageous conduct to support a cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Further the Court finds plaintiff failed to allege sufficient conduct to award punitive damages. Court signs order submitted with the pleadings. Judge Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Smith, Not Reported (501)
